name: Russia
summary: Intelligence/diplomatic supporter of Iran. Providing US military position data. UNSC veto blocks any resolution on the conflict. Urals crude surging alongside global benchmarks ($123.45). OPEC+ 206K bpd increase is nominal. Pravda reported Russia 'poised to ease global helium crunch' (March 30) but no confirmed supply deals as of Day 38. Benefits massively from high oil prices.

signals.content: Russia and China raised diplomatic voices against US-Israeli attacks. Coordinated emergency UNSC sessions. Condemned as sovereignty violations.
Russia providing intelligence to Iran including data on US military positions. Logistical support. But no direct military intervention.

Russia remains an intelligence/diplomatic supporter of Iran. Pravda reported Russia 'poised to ease global helium crunch' (March 30) but no confirmed supply deals have materialized as of Day 38. Urals crude at $123.45 — Russian benchmark surging alongside global crude. Russia's OPEC+ participation in the 206K bpd increase is nominal. Russia's UN Security Council veto power blocks any UNSC resolution on the conflict. No direct military involvement but strategic posture unchanged.

Russia friendly-flag in Iran's three-tier Hormuz system per Bloomberg Tracker — vessels flagged to Russia continue to transit. Russia voted with OPEC+ for the April 5 206K bpd May increase (designed to flood markets the moment Hormuz reopens). Russia is structurally aligned with Iran on shielding the resistance axis but quietly aligned with Saudi/UAE on post-war pumping. The bifurcation lets Russia profit either way: war = high oil prices for Russian exports; peace = aggressive market share post-reopening.

edges.context: Providing US military position data. Diplomatic backing at UNSC. UNSC veto blocks any resolution. But no troops or weapons. Strategic caution.
Joint UNSC actions. Both condemning strikes. Both providing limited support but no military involvement.
Russia benefits from high oil prices as major energy exporter. Urals at $123.45. War increasing Russian oil revenues despite sanctions.
Russia reported 'poised to ease global helium crunch' (Pravda March 30) but no confirmed deals as of April 6. If materialized, could partially offset Qatar's 30-38% offline.
